rulings.Case Title: JOANNE MARIE ANNUZZI et al VS. HENRY FONG PAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Inspection Of Property
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 10, 2015, line 1. PLAINTIFFs JOANNE ANNUZZI and OSVALDO MARTINEZ's Motion To Compel Inspection Of Property Transferred to Department 301 to be heard at 9:00 a.m. on September 25, 2015 with other pending Discovery motion. Parties are ordered to provide courtesy copies of all relevant filings directly to Department 301 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date and time no later than September 18, 2015. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL GALINDO et al VS. SECURITY TITLE INSURANCE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions Of Plaintiffs April Campbell, John Adams, Michael Bennett, Shawn Buckmaster, Joseph Catone, Burdette Green, Anthony Hinson, And Terri Jones, And Request For Monetary And Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 10, 2015, line 2. DEFENDANT CIVIC CENTER HOTEL LLC's Motion To Compel Depositions Of Plaintiffs April Campbell, John Adams, Michael Bennett, Shawn Buckmaster, Joseph Catone, Burdette Green, Anthony Hinson, And Terri Jones, And Request For Monetary And Terminating Sanctions Continued to September 18, 2015 to be heard in Department 301 at 9:00 a.m. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B and provide courtesy copy of reply to Department 301 no later than September 10, 2015 by 4:00 p.m. with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date and place. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES LIU, M.D. VS. YORK H. LIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Confirming Sale Of Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 10, 2015, line 3. OTHER WARREN WONG's Amended Motion For Order Confirming Sale Of Real Property GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to Confirm sale and to confirm broker's commission is GRANTED. Motion to approve referee in attorney fees is DENIED without prejudice to be re-filed after Final Report is made. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARK A. MENDAROS VS. FIRST ALLIED FUNDING ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 10, 2015, line 4. DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action for slander of title, accounting and intentional infliction of emotional distress, non-opposition filed. (Opposition 2:26-27). Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to causes of action "to set aside foreclosure sale", "breach of contract", "quiet title", "cancel trustee sale" for Plaintiff to allege facts re: invalid substitution of trustee in light of RJN and Herrera v. Federal Nat. Mortg. Assn. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 1495. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARK A. MENDAROS VS. FIRST ALLIED FUNDING ET AL

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 10, 2015, line 4. DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY's Motion To Strike Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint GRANTED, non-opposition filed. (Opposition 2:27-28). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SHAOMING CHEN ET AL VS. SHARON YVETTE CROSS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Esponses To Deiscovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 10, 2015, line 8. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Discovery GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant shall provide complete responses by personal delivery without objections to Form Interrogatories-UD, Special Interrogatories and Request for Production within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Admissions are deemed admitted. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1,260. = (501/REQ)
